postData.contentHtml:
In a thrilling World Cup Group G encounter, Belgium delivered a powerful statement with a resounding 5-1 win over New Zealand, firmly establishing themselves as the leaders of their group. The Red Devils' offensive prowess was on full display, with Leandro Trossard netting an impressive brace. Further goals from midfield maestro Kevin De Bruyne, prolific striker Romelu Lukaku, and dynamic winger Alexis Saelemaekers underscored Belgium's dominance.

New Zealand, despite facing an uphill battle, managed to find a consolation goal through Elijah Just, providing a brief moment of celebration for the All Whites. However, it was Belgium's day, as their cohesive play and clinical finishing proved too much for the New Zealand side.

This decisive victory was crucial for Belgium, ensuring they finished first in Group G. Social media sentiment surrounding the match was largely positive, reflecting appreciation for Belgium's strong performance and sparking various discussions among fans about the game's key moments. This result sets the tone for Belgium's continued journey in the expanded 2026 World Cup, which now features 48 teams and additional knockout rounds, promising more exciting football action.
postData.title: Belgium Dominates New Zealand 5-1 to Secure Top Spot in World Cup Group G
postData.summary: Belgium showcased a commanding performance against New Zealand in the World Cup Group G match, clinching a decisive 5-1 victory and securing their place at the top of the group.

sources.title: New Zealand vs. Belgium Highlights: Belgium Routs New Zealand To Top Group G
